overview: This Boros Midrange deck leverages efficient red and white threats to apply steady pressure while outlasting aggressive strategies. By cycling through high-impact creatures and combat tricks, it aims to stabilize early and close games with overwhelming board presence. The strategy relies on reading opponent interactions and maximizing value from each card played.
win_conditions: Deploying Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to steal key opponent cards and attack immediately with haste.
Generating relentless token pressure and card advantage through Ocelot Pride's triggered abilities.
Finishing games with Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury by combat tricking it into lethal damage or using it as a resilient blocker.

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more lands and at least one early threat or card draw
Hands containing Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer paired with a land drop
Hands with Ocelot Pride and a way to trigger its ability or protect it
Keep hands with 2–3 lands (ensuring at least one white and one red source), Ragavan, or early interaction.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with zero lands or four or more lands
Hands lacking any creatures or interactive spells
Hands with only high-cost finishers like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury without early plays
Mulligan pure land, no Ragavan/Ocelot/Phlage, or unplayable mana bases.
In a 3+ land mulligan, prioritize hands with Ragavan or a turn-2 Ocelot Pride setup.

early_game: Play lands consistently to avoid mana screw
Deploy low-cost threats to establish board presence
Use card draw to find answers to early aggression
Turn 1: Play a land, hold mana for potential disruption or Ragavan.
Turn 2: Cast Ragavan (1R), tap for a card draw/spark targeting Ocelot Pride or a cantrip.
Turn 3: Cast Ocelot Pride (1W) if available, begin generating 1/1 Cat Warrior tokens.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast Ragavan, Ocelot Pride, and ramp to Phlage’s 4RR (or 3RR with a Dragon).
mid_game: Transition to Ocelot Pride to generate tokens and stabilize
Trade efficiently to preserve card advantage
Begin setting up Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer for tempo swings
Treat Ragavan as a value engine, not a finisher
Use Ocelot Pride’s ability to generate blockers for stabilization or attackers for pressure.
Manage mana to keep Phlage’s alternate cost open if you control a Dragon, otherwise save for full cost.
Track life total carefully due to Ragavan’s life loss.
Commit threats when you have removal or blockers ready to handle sweepers.
late_game: Deploy Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury as a combat trick or finisher
Use stolen cards from Ragavan to disrupt opponent plans
Maintain board control while pushing for lethal damage
Phlage, Titan of Fire’s Fury is the primary win condition, entering with +1/+1 counters and threatening lethal.
Ocelot Pride’s token swarm can close games if left unblocked or if you can protect it with removal.
Go all-in when you have board dominance, the opponent has no sweepers or spot removal, or you can protect Phlage with counterspells or evasion.
Commit to attacking with tokens when the opponent is forced to block, leaving Phlage open.

key_cards.name: Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to attack immediately. Use its steal ability to remove key opponent spells or threats, then reposition for combat.

key_cards.name: Ocelot Pride
key_cards.notes: Trigger its ability whenever possible to create tokens that can block, push damage, or sacrifice for value. Protect it early to maximize output.

key_cards.name: Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury
key_cards.notes: Hold up mana to cast it in response to combat damage or removal. Use its power to trade up or close out games efficiently.

sequencing_tips: Hold back removal or counterspells until you see what the opponent is protecting.
Cast Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer before committing other threats to maximize its steal potential.
Use Ocelot Pride tokens to pump larger creatures or block efficiently before committing the Pride itself.
Delay casting Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury until combat or after opponent's main phase to avoid wasting it on board wipes.
Phlage’s alternate cost requires controlling a Dragon
include a cheap one like Dragon Fodder if you plan to rely on it.
Ocelot Pride makes Cat Warriors, not Dragons, so don’t confuse the two.
Avoid overcommitting to Ragavan sparks when ahead, and watch your life total.
In multiplayer formats, consider targeting players with the most resources to prevent snowballing.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early blockers and card draw to outlast their initial rush. Use Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to steal their key finishers.
Apply steady pressure with Ocelot Pride tokens to force early removal. Save Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury for after their sweepers resolve.
Race efficiently while trading up with combat tricks. Use Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer to disrupt their game plan and gain tempo.

Use Ragavan’s multiple casting steps to dig through counters. Pair cheap threats with protection or discard to disrupt the opponent’s hand. Force counterspells on non-critical spells to open up for Phlage.

Apply early pressure to force trades before they stabilize. Use removal on key ramp pieces (e.g., Thran Dynamo, Arcane Signet). Stabilize with Ocelot Pride tokens and sweepers once they reach their power level.

Win through superior card advantage and sequencing. Use targeted removal to eliminate key threats like Ocelot Pride or Phlage. Out-value with better mana management and Ragavan sparks. Control the board with sweepers when the opponent overcommits.

common_threats: Fast one-mana creatures that apply early pressure
Board wipes that clear token boards
Card draw spells that outvalue midrange strategies
Watch for sweepers (Amulet Titan’s Thragtusk/Trinisphere, Jeskai Blink’s counters/wipes).
Note if the opponent is trying to blink or counter your key pieces, and adjust your attack timing accordingly.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype: Boros Midrange, Format: modern, Colors: W/R

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards: Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, Ocelot Pride, Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: General Modern midrange piloting principles applied to W/R signature cards

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard Modern format dynamics inferred from W/R midrange archetype
